name: Spinatsuppe (Spinach Soup)
recipeIngredient: 2 quart Chicken stock, fresh or canned
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the fresh spinach thoroughly under cold running water to remove any sand. Drain by shaking it vigorously by hand or in a lettuce basket, then chop coarsely.
2. If frozen spinach is used, thaw and drain it thoroughly.
3. Bring 2 quarts of chicken stock to a boil in a 3-4 quart saucepan. Add the fresh or frozen chopped spinach. Simmer uncovered about 6 to 8 minutes.
4. Pour the contents into a sieve set over a large bowl. Press down hard on the spinach with the back of a wooden spoon to extract all juices. Set liquid aside in the bowl and chop cooked spinach very fine.
5. Melt 3 tablespoons of butter in the saucepan. When foam subsides, remove pan from heat and stir in flour. With a wire whisk, beat hot stock into this white roux a little at a time.
6. Return saucepan to heat and, stirring constantly, bring mixture to boil. Add spinach. Season soup with salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
7. Half cover the pan and simmer soup over low heat about 5 minutes longer. Stir occasionally.
8. Garnish each serving of soup with a few slices of hard-cooked egg. On festive occasions, serve with stuffed eggs half floating in each bowl. Roll mixture into balls and nestle 2-3 into each halved egg white.
